title: Transfer Options
description: Shared configuration block for blind and warm transfer choreography, including destination targeting, caller handling, confirmation gates, and failure dispositions. Reused by the transfer verb and the handoff component.

mode.description: Transfer mode: 'blind' hands off immediately without waiting for the destination to accept; 'warm' allows the agent to brief the destination before connecting the caller.

blindMethod.description: blind only: 'refer' sends a SIP REFER to the remote end; 'dial' places a bridged outbound call. Not auto-detected — must be set explicitly when the default 'refer' is not appropriate.

callerPresent.description: warm only: when true the caller is added to a three-way conference and can hear the brief; when false the caller is parked (on hold) and does not hear the conversation between agent and destination.

target.description: One or more call destinations. All targets are rung simultaneously; the first to answer wins.

callerId.description: Caller ID presented to the transfer destination.

onHoldHook.description: warm/parked only: verbs to execute for the parked caller while the agent briefs the destination (e.g. play hold music). Only invoked when callerPresent is false.

timeout.description: Seconds to wait for the destination to answer before applying the onNoAnswer disposition.

confirm.description: Optional human-side acceptance gate. The destination hears a prompt and must press the specified digit to accept. A wrong digit, timeout, or hangup is treated as a 'decline' disposition.

prompt.description: Text or URL of the prompt played to the destination.

digit.description: Single DTMF digit the destination must press to accept the transfer.

amd.description: warm only: optional answering-machine detection run on the destination leg. Off by default; a 'machine' result is treated as a 'decline' disposition.

disposition.description: Per-outcome fallback actions when the transfer does not complete normally.

onNoAnswer.description: Action when the destination does not answer within timeout.

onBusy.description: Action when the destination returns a busy signal.

onDecline.description: Action when the destination declines the transfer (confirm gate failed, AMD detected machine, or explicit rejection).

onFailure.description: Action when a protocol-level error occurs on the destination leg.

voicemailUrl.description: SIP URI or HTTP URL to route the caller to voicemail. Required when any disposition value is 'voicemail'.

actionHook.description: Fires when the transfer resolves — whether the call was bridged, returned to the caller, sent to voicemail, or failed.

eventHook.description: Optional hook for in-progress transfer events (e.g. destination ringing, brief started).
